hero.bioP1: 6+ years building developer platforms. I'm part of QuintoAndar Group's Platform Engineering team, focused on our IDP (Backstage.io) and building tooling that improves developer experience and productivity.
hero.quote: "So others may live." — U.S. Coast Guard Rescue Swimmer

ping.title: ping --help
ping.sub: think I could help you crack a problem?

doctrine.manifestoP1Post: . Usually, the less we're noticed and the more productive teams are, the better we're doing.
doctrine.manifestoP2Pre: The first platform team I worked with had this motto:
doctrine.manifestoP2Post: — we do the complex stuff so others don't have to, we go where others wouldn't, to do what they wouldn't — without anyone noticing we exist.
doctrine.manifestoP3Pre: That's exactly what platform is — and it ties straight back to DevOps culture. At the end of the day, what I love most is helping other engineers solve problems
doctrine.manifestoP4: TL;DR: if we can make the best path the easiest path, with the best tools, for everyone — our platform mission is accomplished.

roles.name: DevOps
roles.text: DevOps to me is culture, not tooling or a job title. It's a daily habit: shortening the feedback loop between who builds and who runs, until both sides stop existing as sides. When dev and ops share the same pager, the code comes out different.

roles.name: SRE
roles.text: SRE is the engineer who treats operations as software: SLO before code, toil measured in hours, incident turning into postmortem and postmortem into real change. On-call is an excellent way to learn how systems actually work, find improvement opportunities and observability gaps.

roles.name: Platform Engineer
roles.text: That's when all of this turns into product: self-service, paved roads, abstractions that protect the dev without locking them in. Favorite metric: time others gained without noticing. The more invisible, the better.

ping.intro: Platform is teamwork. If you want to swap notes, bring a talk to your team, or you've got a project that needs an extra brain — the inbox is open.

ctas.title: swap notes
ctas.desc: chat about Platform Engineering / DevEx — how it's going on your team, what works, what hurts.

ctas.title: bring a talk
ctas.desc: have one of my talks at your team or event — Backstage, IDP, AI ops, RAG, observability.

ctas.title: partner on a project
ctas.desc: got a project that needs an extra brain or hands-on work? call me in to design it together.

about.hobbies: Off-hours: ML Eng grad school, MBA in AI Eng + Multi-agents, pastoral theology course. Mango Monster, a podcast on the train, a Friday-night ARAM.

sidequests.footerNote: · new projects show up here as they leave the napkin

items.name: 3Dantas
items.tagline: filament craft — made-to-order pieces + own line.
items.bodyP1: 3D printing brand running on a Bambu Lab A1 mini. Orders (custom Funkos, keychains, trophies, sensory toys) come via WhatsApp; in parallel, the Funkos Santos line lives in the catalog.
items.bodyP2: Metric: sustainable hobby — operation pays for ≥50% of any reinvestment.

items.name: Novarum
items.tagline: tooling for altar servers, EJC and Pascom — taking weight off logistics.
items.bodyP1: Technical arm of my volunteer work in the Catholic Church. Building tools to replace spreadsheets + WhatsApp + improvised processes, so the ministry can focus on people, not logistics.
items.bodyP2: Audience: altar servers (coroinhas), EJC (Youth Encounter with Christ), Pascom (Pastoral Communication). Near-zero hosting.

items.name: DeployOu
items.tagline: brazilian dev humor — DevOps puns on cotton.
items.bodyP1: T-shirt brand via Reserva INK (no inventory management). Brazilian dev humor: Kubernetes, SRE, Cloud Native puns. Direct, ironic, complicit tone — talks to people who get the joke without explanation.
items.bodyP2: Audience: devs, SREs, platform engineers — folks who laugh at their own field. Autopilot mode operation.

writing.emptyState: Medium feed offline. New posts will show up here automatically.
writing.readmeBody: Everything here is pulled at build time from the medium/@_gdantas RSS feed. Topics: AI ops, dev platform, observability, incident agents, RAG over infra. Every new Medium post shows up in this list automatically.
